subject: LU-7655 tests: ost fake write for performance testing

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 2:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 2:

Why not use obdfilter-survey to generate a test load for this?

Patch Set 2:

Hi Andreas,

For this patch it will just discard writing pages so I expect the writing speed should super fast. I'm not sure what obdfilter-survey can do, I will take a look at it.

Patch Set 2:

Ah, I thought you were using this to generate IO load.  The obdfilter script + lctl can generate the IO pages for OFD, but your patch is discarding the IO before it is sent to the disk, which isn't the intention of obdfilter-survey at all.  

obdfilter-survey _can_ run on the client against "echo_server", which also discards the pages on the server, but that happens before it gets to OFD I think.

Patch Set 5: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

It looks like this functionality is duplicated in http://review.whamcloud.com/4389 and that patch also adds some testing and usage of this code to reduce test times.  It would be good to get one of these patches landed.

Patch Set 5: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

one issue with the approach taken is that disk usage doesn't change. with ldiskfs it's kind of easy to skip writes given we control I/O path, so can skip specific I/O requests. but that's not the case with ZFS..

Patch Set 5:

"so can skip specific I/O requests. but that's not the case with ZFS.."

why do I need to care about disk usage?

Patch Set 5:

One advantage with this patch is that we can write 100G data to a 4G ramdisk, this is a good thing to exercise memory cache on client.

Patch Set 6: (2 inline comments)

actually I'm wondering why do we maintain size in osd*.. especially given DMU has no this notion. woudn't it be better to maintain it in OFD, so that your code in OFD can replace copies in different OSDs.

Patch Set 10: Code-Review-1

Jinshan, it would be great if you could refresh this patch, but using a new fail_loc = OBD_FAIL_OST_BRW_FAKE, so that it isn't exposed to userspace like a /proc tunable is.  

This would be something that we could land and use for testing client or network performance limits without needing fast disks.

commit.subject: LU-7655 tests: ost fake write for performance testing
commit.message:
LU-7655 tests: ost fake write for performance testing

Just drop the pages in ofd_commitrw_write(), but we need to maintain
correct file size and always create a transaction so client can pin
those pages in memory until transaction commits.

Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ia9a2af0a159c8969479656d3a7016db3cda71a91
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/5164
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
